signals.title: Claude Code hooks can add context before prompt processing
signals.summary: Claude Code hooks include UserPromptSubmit, which can add extra context before the user prompt is processed.
signals.impact: Pre-plan context injection can be implemented outside the main agent loop in clients that support prompt hooks.
signals.why_it_matters: Newswiki's pre-plan brief can later become an automatic hook integration, but v1 should prove that the brief itself improves decisions.
signals.decision_impact: Separate brief quality from automation convenience in the roadmap.
signals.source_claim: Claude Code hooks include UserPromptSubmit, which can add extra context before the user prompt is processed.
signals.newswiki_interpretation: Newswiki's pre-plan brief can later become an automatic hook integration, but v1 should prove that the brief itself improves decisions.

signals.title: Claude Code supports remote MCP servers
signals.summary: Anthropic announced remote MCP support in Claude Code so agents can access third-party tools, project systems, and knowledge bases without local server management.
signals.impact: Remote MCP is already an accepted Claude Code integration path.
signals.why_it_matters: A hosted Newswiki MCP can be tested as a user-facing connector instead of only as a local template.
signals.decision_impact: Keep Claude Code setup prompts and smoke tests in the repo, but position them as client validation rather than the product itself.
signals.source_claim: Anthropic announced remote MCP support in Claude Code so agents can access third-party tools, project systems, and knowledge bases without local server management.
signals.newswiki_interpretation: A hosted Newswiki MCP can be tested as a user-facing connector instead of only as a local template.

signals.title: Claude Code subagents can inherit MCP tools
signals.summary: Claude Code docs say subagents can inherit MCP tools from the main conversation unless tool access is restricted.
signals.impact: A single Newswiki context service can support main agents and specialized reviewers when clients expose MCP tools to subagents.
signals.why_it_matters: Cross-agent context becomes more useful when specialized review, research, and implementation agents can query the same source-backed brief layer.
signals.decision_impact: Design Newswiki brief outputs to be self-contained enough for subagents with separate context windows.
signals.source_claim: Claude Code docs say subagents can inherit MCP tools from the main conversation unless tool access is restricted.
signals.newswiki_interpretation: Cross-agent context becomes more useful when specialized review, research, and implementation agents can query the same source-backed brief layer.

signals.title: Cloudflare proposes Code Mode to reduce MCP tool context cost
signals.summary: Cloudflare's Code Mode pattern exposes a large API through a compact server-side interface with progressive discovery and sandboxed execution.
signals.impact: The MCP ecosystem is optimizing for fewer exposed tools and more server-side composition.
signals.why_it_matters: Newswiki should expose a small number of high-level brief/search tools instead of many narrow signal tools.
signals.decision_impact: Keep `get_context_for_task` as the flagship high-level tool.
signals.source_claim: Cloudflare's Code Mode pattern exposes a large API through a compact server-side interface with progressive discovery and sandboxed execution.
signals.newswiki_interpretation: Newswiki should expose a small number of high-level brief/search tools instead of many narrow signal tools.

signals.title: Cloudflare frames MCP adoption as an enterprise security problem
signals.summary: Cloudflare describes company-wide MCP adoption and the need to manage security risks such as authorization sprawl, prompt injection, and supply-chain exposure.
signals.impact: Hosted MCP services need governance and minimal disclosure by design.
signals.why_it_matters: Newswiki's public-safe boundary, source URLs, and data-limit warnings are product requirements, not polish.
signals.decision_impact: Prioritize read-only context and validation over broad write-back MCP features.
signals.source_claim: Cloudflare describes company-wide MCP adoption and the need to manage security risks such as authorization sprawl, prompt injection, and supply-chain exposure.
signals.newswiki_interpretation: Newswiki's public-safe boundary, source URLs, and data-limit warnings are product requirements, not polish.

signals.title: Codex expands native memory, plugins, and automations
signals.summary: OpenAI's April 2026 Codex update adds memory preview, more than 90 plugins, expanded automations, and broader desktop workflow support.
signals.impact: Native agent platforms are absorbing generic memory, plugin, and workflow infrastructure.
signals.why_it_matters: Newswiki should avoid selling a generic skill index or memory layer as the moat; the wedge is current, source-backed external intelligence.
signals.decision_impact: Keep Capability MCP on-demand and make external signal quality the primary product proof.
signals.source_claim: OpenAI's April 2026 Codex update adds memory preview, more than 90 plugins, expanded automations, and broader desktop workflow support.
signals.newswiki_interpretation: Newswiki should avoid selling a generic skill index or memory layer as the moat; the wedge is current, source-backed external intelligence.
data_limits: Automated fetch may be blocked; manually verify the source page before treating it as current.

signals.title: Cursor reports token savings from dynamic MCP context discovery
signals.summary: Cursor's dynamic context discovery post reports a 46.9% token reduction in MCP-tool runs by exposing compact tool context and prompting lookup only when needed.
signals.impact: Context systems should route and compress information instead of dumping all tools and knowledge into every prompt.
signals.why_it_matters: This directly supports Newswiki's move away from always-on Capability MCP and toward task-relevant pre-plan briefs.
signals.decision_impact: Default to compact retrieval decisions and explicit skipped layers.
signals.source_claim: Cursor's dynamic context discovery post reports a 46.9% token reduction in MCP-tool runs by exposing compact tool context and prompting lookup only when needed.
signals.newswiki_interpretation: This directly supports Newswiki's move away from always-on Capability MCP and toward task-relevant pre-plan briefs.

signals.title: Cursor packages MCP servers, skills, subagents, rules, and hooks as plugins
signals.summary: Cursor's plugin announcement presents plugins as bundles of MCP servers, skills, subagents, rules, and hooks for agent workflows.
signals.impact: Agent clients are starting to own capability packaging and distribution.
signals.why_it_matters: A standalone Newswiki skill registry is likely to be commoditized unless it is tied to stronger context and signal workflows.
signals.decision_impact: Treat skills as optional adapters or recommendations, not the core v1 product.
signals.source_claim: Cursor's plugin announcement presents plugins as bundles of MCP servers, skills, subagents, rules, and hooks for agent workflows.
signals.newswiki_interpretation: A standalone Newswiki skill registry is likely to be commoditized unless it is tied to stronger context and signal workflows.

signals.title: Gemini CLI adds subagents with separate tools and context
signals.summary: Google announced Gemini CLI subagents that operate with their own tools, MCP servers, instructions, and context windows.
signals.impact: Subagent architectures are becoming cross-platform, increasing the need for shared, compact context packages.
signals.why_it_matters: Newswiki should not be Claude- or Codex-specific; its output contract should work for any agent that can read a brief or call MCP.
signals.decision_impact: Keep the context pack schema platform-neutral and avoid depending on one client's memory system.
signals.source_claim: Google announced Gemini CLI subagents that operate with their own tools, MCP servers, instructions, and context windows.
signals.newswiki_interpretation: Newswiki should not be Claude- or Codex-specific; its output contract should work for any agent that can read a brief or call MCP.

signals.title: Copilot coding agent adds semantic code search
signals.summary: GitHub says Copilot coding agent now has semantic code search and automatically uses it when appropriate.
signals.impact: Agent runtimes are adding better automatic retrieval for repository context.
signals.why_it_matters: Newswiki should focus on external and cross-project signals that platform-local code search cannot provide.
signals.decision_impact: Do not position Newswiki as a replacement for client-native code search.
signals.source_claim: GitHub says Copilot coding agent now has semantic code search and automatically uses it when appropriate.
signals.newswiki_interpretation: Newswiki should focus on external and cross-project signals that platform-local code search cannot provide.

signals.title: GitHub MCP expands toward HTTP OAuth and Copilot agent tooling
signals.summary: GitHub's MCP server update adds HTTP server mode with OAuth token support and new tools for working with Copilot coding agent jobs.
signals.impact: GitHub is treating MCP as part of hosted agent operations, not just local integration.
signals.why_it_matters: Newswiki should assume coding agents will increasingly run in hosted surfaces and need compact, authenticated, source-backed context.
signals.decision_impact: Do not overfit Newswiki to one local client config format.
signals.source_claim: GitHub's MCP server update adds HTTP server mode with OAuth token support and new tools for working with Copilot coding agent jobs.
signals.newswiki_interpretation: Newswiki should assume coding agents will increasingly run in hosted surfaces and need compact, authenticated, source-backed context.

signals.title: Hermes Agent exposes memory, skills, and MCP integration as native agent capabilities
signals.summary: Hermes official documentation presents persistent memory, a skills system, and MCP integration as built-in capabilities for the agent runtime.
signals.impact: Generic memory, skill, and MCP wrapper features are less differentiated when agent runtimes provide them natively.
signals.why_it_matters: Newswiki should complement Hermes with current source-backed external context instead of competing as another memory or skill runtime.
signals.decision_impact: Support Hermes as a consumer of Newswiki briefs, but defer Hermes-specific write-back or skill-curator integration until the brief proves value.
signals.source_claim: Hermes documentation describes persistent memory, skills, tools, and MCP integration as core features.
signals.newswiki_interpretation: Newswiki should treat Hermes as a complementary agent client rather than a capability layer to replace.

signals.title: MCP roadmap calls out enterprise readiness gaps
signals.summary: The MCP roadmap highlights audit trails, SSO-integrated auth, gateway behavior, and configuration portability as enterprise readiness needs.
signals.impact: Enterprise MCP products need governance and explainability, not only tool wrappers.
signals.why_it_matters: Newswiki's boundary design should make source provenance, user-visible data limits, and auth expectations first-class product surfaces.
signals.decision_impact: Keep public-safe exports and source-backed briefs as the safety boundary for hosted alpha.
signals.source_claim: The MCP roadmap highlights audit trails, SSO-integrated auth, gateway behavior, and configuration portability as enterprise readiness needs.
signals.newswiki_interpretation: Newswiki's boundary design should make source provenance, user-visible data limits, and auth expectations first-class product surfaces.

signals.title: MCP roadmap prioritizes remote transport scalability
signals.summary: The 2026 MCP roadmap names transport evolution and horizontal scalability as priority work for production remote MCP servers.
signals.impact: Hosted MCP services should plan for stateless scaling, session handling, and discoverable server metadata.
signals.why_it_matters: A context service that assumes local stdio-only MCP will age poorly as the protocol moves further into production remote deployments.
signals.decision_impact: Prefer a read-only remote MCP contract with explicit provenance and health metadata before adding write paths.
signals.source_claim: The 2026 MCP roadmap names transport evolution and horizontal scalability as priority work for production remote MCP servers.
signals.newswiki_interpretation: A context service that assumes local stdio-only MCP will age poorly as the protocol moves further into production remote deployments.

signals.title: Vercel ships an official remote MCP server
signals.summary: Vercel documents an official OAuth-based remote MCP endpoint at mcp.vercel.com for project, deployment, log, and documentation workflows.
signals.impact: Major developer platforms are packaging operational context as remote MCP services across multiple agents.
signals.why_it_matters: Newswiki should compete on curated external intelligence and brief quality, not on the novelty of exposing an MCP endpoint.
signals.decision_impact: Use Vercel-style OAuth remote MCP as a comparable deployment pattern, while differentiating through signal curation.
signals.source_claim: Vercel documents an official OAuth-based remote MCP endpoint at mcp.vercel.com for project, deployment, log, and documentation workflows.
signals.newswiki_interpretation: Newswiki should compete on curated external intelligence and brief quality, not on the novelty of exposing an MCP endpoint.
